name: Pee Wee Russell
abstract: Charles Ellsworth "Pee Wee" Russell (March 27, 1906 – February 15, 1969) was an American jazz musician. Early in his career he played clarinet and saxophones, but he eventually focused solely on clarinet.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Dody Goodman in "Dody in Dixieland"
Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: And then one time they had a band coming to the Ken Club in Boston with Pee Wee Russell and Max Kaminsky and Miff Mole. And they needed a Boston rhythm section... I was playing seven nights and Sunday afternoon for four or six weeks... work with Pee Wee Russell
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Condon brought Pee Wee Russell to Disneyland the year I didn't go. I never heard either one of them in person.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: he and Pee Wee Russell were very good friends... I think he made some records... with Pee Wee Russell

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: I was highly influenced by Pee Wee Russell quite a bit. I always enjoyed his playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: we had a little group called the Newport Jazz Festival All Stars, which was Ruby Braff... Pee Wee Russell, clarinet, George played the piano... And we played -- did a lot of dates around the States

about_quotes.speaker: Mat Domber
about_quotes.evidence: Pee Wee Russell was one of my idols. When I was 13 years old I studied the clarinet for one year and I used to sit on the edge of my bed in the room trying to emulate Pee Wee Russell’s solos
about_quotes.doc_title: Mat Domber interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007
